name: Sea of Thieves: 2026 Edition
description: Sea of Thieves is a smash-hit pirate adventure game, offering the quintessential pirate experience of plundering lost treasures, intense battles, vanquishing sea monsters and more. Dive in with this revised edition of the game, which includes access to digital bonus media.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 10 version 18362.0 or higher
  • Processor: Intel i3 4170 @ 3.7 GHz or AMD FX-6300 @ 3.5 GHz
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: Nvidia GeForce GTX 660 or AMD Radeon R9 270
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 100 GB available space
comment1:
I fu-cking love Sea of Thieves. But this adoration comes with a huge caveat: Only when I play it with my friends. 

Playing this game with a full galleon of my favourite people in a Discord call together is some of the most fun I've EVER had in a videogame and I think those moments are genuinely unmatched. So much so that I have 100 hours in this game and maybe 5 of them were just me on my own. So much so that I made TWO hours of YouTube videos of just me and my friends messing around in the game and accomplishing nothing specific. We played so much of this game we have developed specific terminology to describe things in game and our own methodology to completing tasks and farming resources. 

I genuinely adore this game so much and yet at 112GBs of size I just cannot justify reinstalling this game when I can't guarantee that I'll be able to get everyone on board (haha get it?) in a world where Sea of Thieves literally has a paid battlepass and seasonal challenges in a game that costs 40 f-ucking Great British Pounds. 

I love so much about this game, if you are in a group of good friends just looking to mess around and maybe sink a few noobs but at the same time, the rampant and blatant cheaters, absolutely no-life sweats that take you down in 4 seconds flat, and again, incredibly predatory monetisation is very off-putting. 

I give this game so many passes but new content recently has been so sparse and flavourless that it really is hard to endorse as a returning player. 

I have marked this game as recommended but please Rare, Microsoft, whoever is at the helm (heheh GET IT GET IT?) please sort out the constant teleporting pirate ninjas and subpar updates.
comment2: The devs supported a pdf file. The community managers knew about it and covered it up.
comment3: Community manager protected a predator and even uplifted them to a position where they could more easily access and exploit minors. Situation was being so badly mishandled that official partners are stepping down. Abhorrent situation and a dismal response to it by Rare.
